title: Fermented Crispy Saudi Lettuce Salad
description: A nostalgic Saudi Arabian salad that blends the tang of fermented labneh, the crunch of tandoor-roasted chickpeas, and crisp romaine lettuce into a refreshing dish.

ingredients.name: Romaine lettuce leaves
ingredients.notes: washed and torn into bite-sized pieces

instructions: Preheat the tandoor or oven to 200°C (392°F).

Place the dry chickpeas on a baking sheet, drizzle with a tablespoon of olive oil, and season with cumin, sumac, and a pinch of salt. Roast in the tandoor for 10–12 minutes until golden and crisp, stirring once. Remove and let cool slightly.
In a large bowl, combine the torn romaine lettuce, diced cucumber, shredded carrot, and thinly sliced red onion. Toss gently to mix.
In a separate bowl, whisk together fermented labneh, fresh lemon juice, a tablespoon of olive oil, sumac, cumin, black pepper, and salt until smooth and creamy.
Drizzle the dressing over the salad mixture and toss until all ingredients are evenly coated.
Gently fold the roasted chickpeas into the salad, ensuring they remain crisp.
Taste and adjust seasoning with additional salt, pepper, or sumac if desired.
Serve the salad immediately, garnishing with extra sumac or lemon wedges if liked.
